title: rostraria pumila var. glabrescens
intro: Rostraria pumila var. glabrescens, also known as Wood Hair-grass or False Oat-grass, is a type of flowering plant in the grass family (Poaceae). It is primarily used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to aid in wound healing and reducing pain and swelling. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and even acting as a natural diuretic, making it a useful remedy for urinary problems. Rostraria pumila var. glabrescens can be prepared into various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and infusions for topical applications.
